facts.text: Boston was founded on the Shawmut Peninsula in 1630 by English Puritan settlers who named it after Boston, Lincolnshire in England.
The 1629 Cambridge Agreement, signed by John Winthrop, laid the groundwork for Massachusetts Bay Colony before Boston’s settlement even began.
In 1852 the Boston Public Library opened as the first free library in the United States, a landmark for public access to knowledge.
Boston Latin School, founded in 1635, is America’s oldest public school and the starting point for many of the nation’s leaders.
The Back Bay was created by filling three original hills and importing gravel from Needham, turning a marsh into Boston’s elegant neighborhood.
The Boston Celtics, founded in 1946, have won eighteen NBA championships, the most of any team in league history.
